subject: UX & Research
title: The Forgiveness Principle
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Forgiveness Principle.

hook.text: A bank app says 'Transaction failed.' Another says 'Card declined — try another card.' Night and day.

buildup.text: The forgiveness principle: good design prevents errors, but great design helps users recover when errors happen.

discovery.text: Helpful error messages include three things: what went wrong, why it happened, and how to fix it.

twist.text: GitHub's 404 page has a search bar and popular links. It turns a dead end into a helpful redirect.

climax.text: Every error is a chance to help. Design error states as carefully as success states.

punchline.text: Errors happen. How you handle them defines the experience.

quiz.question: What three things should a good error message include?
options: What went wrong, why it happened, and how to fix it
An apology, a discount code, and a link to support
Technical error codes, timestamps, and server logs
